assets.name: lecture_06_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: This is the clearest of the four candidates for note-writing purposes. The lecturer has moved enough that the central interaction sketch is less occluded than in the earlier frames, while the labeled charged-particle lines and the nearby energy note on the right remain readable. It preserves both the board diagram and the accompanying mass-scale annotation, which makes it more useful than a cleaner-but-emptier lecturer shot.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: I'll just draw you some simple diagrams

assets.name: lecture_06_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the best overall board evidence. The central asymmetry expression is clearly readable, the left-hand interaction sketch is preserved with less occlusion than in the other candidates, and the partial large equation plus numbered list on the right remain visible enough to document the board layout at this moment.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: look symmetric going forward and backward.

assets.name: lecture_06_figure_04.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the best balanced view of the board: the central and upper-right asymmetry equations are legible, the numbered checklist with check marks is preserved on the right, and the left-hand interaction sketch remains visible. The lecturer’s posture causes minimal additional occlusion compared with the neighboring frames, making this the most useful documentary screenshot for later note reconstruction.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: actual history of the universe was symmetric.
